steps.title: Find the key signal — what makes the contrast effective
steps.content: The statement says the student was surprised the Amiga sold “relatively few” units. This surprise only makes sense if we compare the Amiga to a system that sold vastly more. The table shows Amiga: 1,600,000 units. To emphasize how “relatively few” that is, we need the highest sales figure available — Atari 2600 at 18,450,000 units, which is more than 11 times higher.

steps.title: Match the strongest contrast to the choices
steps.content: Choice A pairs Amiga's correct figure (1,600,000) with Atari 2600's correct figure (18,450,000), creating the dramatic contrast needed to explain the student's surprise. Choices B and C misidentify the Amiga's sales (using 3,935,000, which belongs to Sega), and choice D incorrectly pairs 18,450,000 with the wrong system. That matches choice A — A is correct.
